# Stdio and Streamable HTTP expose one shared MCP registry beside REST and the web UI.
env MINA_FX_AUTO_LOAD_ENABLED=false
freeport
exec mina serve --demo --db $WORK/mcp.db --yes --host 127.0.0.1 --port $PORT --quiet &server&
httpwait -timeout 15s '"status":"ok"' http://127.0.0.1:$PORT/api/health

# Stdio lists representative tools and leaves annotation assertions to the script.
mcplist stdio http://127.0.0.1:$PORT
stdout '^transport=stdio tool=transactions_list read_only=true destructive=false idempotent=true open_world=false$'
stdout '^transport=stdio tool=accounts_delete read_only=false destructive=true idempotent=true open_world=false$'
stdout '^transport=stdio tool=accounts_get '
stdout '^transport=stdio tool=members_create '

mcpcall stdio http://127.0.0.1:$PORT transactions_list '{"limit":5}'
stdout '"status":200'
stdout '"transactions":\['

# Streamable HTTP lists and calls tools through the same listener.
mcplist http http://127.0.0.1:$PORT/mcp
stdout '^transport=http tool=transactions_list read_only=true destructive=false idempotent=true open_world=false$'
stdout '^transport=http tool=accounts_delete read_only=false destructive=true idempotent=true open_world=false$'

mcpcall http http://127.0.0.1:$PORT/mcp accounts_get '{"account_id":4}'
stdout '"status":200'
stdout '"account_id":4'
stdout '"fqn":"checking:Chase:Joint"'

! mcpcall http http://127.0.0.1:$PORT/mcp members_create '{"body":{"name":""}}'
stderr '"code":"invalid_request"'
stderr '"message"'

mcpcall http http://127.0.0.1:$PORT/mcp members_create '{"body":{"name":"MCP Smoke Member"}}'
stdout '"status":201'
stdout '"member_id":'

# Non-loopback origins stay forbidden on Streamable HTTP.
httpget -method POST -status 403 -accept 'application/json, text/event-stream' -origin https://example.com -body initialize.json http://127.0.0.1:$PORT/mcp
stdout 'Forbidden: Origin must be loopback'

# REST and the web UI remain reachable on the same listener.
httpget http://127.0.0.1:$PORT/api/health
stdout '"status":"ok"'
httpget -accept text/html http://127.0.0.1:$PORT/
stdout '<div id="root"></div>'

kill -INT server
wait server

-- initialize.json --
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2025-06-18","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"origin-probe","version":"test"}}}
